companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: NVIDIA remains the central supplier for AI accelerators and increasingly full-stack AI infrastructure.

items.headline: RTX Spark targets local agentic AI PCs
items.detail: NVIDIA unveiled RTX Spark at Computex 2026, positioning Blackwell-class Windows PCs for local agentic AI workloads.

items.headline: AI demand remained strong in earnings
items.detail: Recent earnings coverage said demand for NVIDIA's AI hardware continued to exceed expectations.

items.headline: Rubin and Blackwell roadmap stays central
items.detail: Jensen Huang emphasized the Blackwell-to-Rubin roadmap and a very large AI infrastructure revenue opportunity.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Google is pushing Gemini across consumer products while scaling TPU and cloud capacity.

items.headline: Gemini agents spread across Google products
items.detail: Google I/O 2026 focused on Gemini agents in Search, Gmail, YouTube, Docs, and Chrome.

items.headline: Google Cloud growth tied to AI demand
items.detail: Google Cloud topped $20B quarterly revenue, with AI demand and capacity constraints central to the story.

items.headline: AI capex race continues
items.detail: Industry coverage projected very large 2026 AI infrastructure spending across Alphabet and other hyperscalers.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Microsoft continues to deepen Copilot, Azure AI, and its own AI stack alongside the OpenAI partnership.

items.headline: Build 2026 centered on agentic AI
items.detail: Microsoft Build 2026 coverage highlighted Azure, GitHub Copilot, Windows development, and responsible AI.

items.headline: Copilot integration expands in Windows
items.detail: Microsoft confirmed plans to integrate Copilot more deeply with the Windows 11 taskbar and Start menu.

items.headline: Microsoft builds more of its own AI stack
items.detail: Coverage described Microsoft's effort to reduce exclusive reliance on OpenAI by expanding internal AI capabilities.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: OpenAI continues to expand ChatGPT, Codex, enterprise deployments, and capital commitments for frontier AI.

items.headline: Major committed-capital round announced
items.detail: OpenAI announced $122B in committed capital at an $852B post-money valuation.

items.headline: Older ChatGPT models are being retired
items.detail: ChatGPT release notes say OpenAI is retiring older models including GPT-4.5 and o3 from ChatGPT.

items.headline: Codex expands into hybrid enterprise environments
items.detail: OpenAI announced a Dell partnership to bring Codex to hybrid and on-prem enterprise environments.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Anthropic is scaling Claude distribution, funding, and enterprise cloud availability.

items.headline: Confidential IPO filing reported
items.detail: Axios reported Anthropic filed confidentially for an IPO after its latest funding round.

items.headline: Series H values Anthropic near $965B
items.detail: Anthropic announced a $65B Series H at a $965B post-money valuation.

items.headline: Claude Opus 4.8 arrives on AWS
items.detail: AWS highlighted Claude Opus 4.8 availability through AWS and Bedrock.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and custom silicon
companies.summary: Meta is pairing Llama-style model work with custom chips and large infrastructure commitments.

items.headline: Meta and Broadcom expand MTIA partnership
items.detail: Meta and Broadcom expanded their custom silicon partnership for Meta's MTIA AI chips.

items.headline: Four new MTIA inference chips revealed
items.detail: Meta revealed four new MTIA chips built for AI inference.

items.headline: Large Amazon Graviton deal signals AI CPU needs
items.detail: Meta's multi-billion-dollar Graviton deal with Amazon highlighted rising CPU demand for agentic AI workloads.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS is expanding model availability, custom AI chips, and large-scale compute partnerships.

items.headline: Claude Opus 4.8 added through AWS
items.detail: AWS added Claude Opus 4.8 availability through Bedrock and related AWS channels.

items.headline: AWS and Cerebras collaborate on inference
items.detail: AWS and Cerebras announced a Bedrock and Trainium collaboration aimed at faster AI inference.

items.headline: Amazon and Anthropic expand compute collaboration
items.detail: Anthropic said its Amazon compute collaboration includes up to 5GW of capacity for Claude training and deployment.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: xAI is scaling Grok and building large data-center capacity around Colossus.

items.headline: xAI announces $20B Series E
items.detail: xAI said it raised $20B in Series E funding to expand Grok and data-center capacity.

items.headline: Colossus 2 debt financing reported
items.detail: Data Center Dynamics reported xAI sought $12B in debt to fund the Colossus 2 data center.

items.headline: Anthropic compute purchase reported
items.detail: TechCrunch reported Anthropic would pay xAI for large-scale compute capacity.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: Broadcom is gaining importance as hyperscalers invest in custom AI silicon and private AI infrastructure.

items.headline: Meta custom silicon partnership expands
items.detail: Broadcom said it expanded its Meta partnership to support multi-gigawatt MTIA custom silicon deployments.

items.headline: VMware Cloud Foundation 9.1 targets AI workloads
items.detail: Broadcom launched VMware Cloud Foundation 9.1 with messaging around production AI, inference, and agentic AI workloads.

items.headline: Custom AI ASIC role draws more attention
items.detail: Industry coverage continued to frame Broadcom as a central custom AI ASIC supplier for hyperscalers.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: Palantir is riding AIP demand across commercial and government buyers while defending key public-sector opportunities.

items.headline: AIP momentum lifted Q1 guidance
items.detail: Palantir's Q1 2026 update showed strong AIP-driven momentum and raised full-year guidance.

items.headline: DIA contract fight escalates
items.detail: Axios reported Palantir is fighting the Pentagon's DIA over a key intelligence data modernization contract.

items.headline: DHS awards large AI and data analytics agreement
items.detail: DHS awarded Palantir a five-year AI and data analytics purchasing agreement worth up to $1B.

ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confidentially submitted a draft Form S-1 to the SEC on June 1, 2026. The SEC review is the next step before any public S-1, roadshow, pricing, or listing.
ipoTracker.note: No share count, price range, ticker, or listing date has been announced.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic confidentially submits draft S-1 to the SEC

ipoTracker.process: Axios and Reuters-syndicated reporting say OpenAI is preparing a confidential IPO prospectus that could be filed soon, but OpenAI has not announced a filing.
ipoTracker.note: Treat this as watchlist status until OpenAI confirms a submission or a public filing appears.

ipoTracker.process: SpaceX is not a pure AI company, but reports and IPO-related coverage say xAI/Grok and AI infrastructure are part of its public-market story.
ipoTracker.note: Track separately from standalone xAI because current reporting ties xAI exposure to SpaceX.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX has listed Grok's spicy mode as a risk in its initial public offering

ipoTracker.process: Cerebras filed its S-1 in April 2026, launched the offering in May 2026, and completed its Nasdaq IPO under ticker CBRS.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed by SEC and company filings
ipoTracker.note: Keep in the tracker as a fresh AI IPO benchmark rather than an upcoming filing.

ipoTracker.process: Databricks remains a major AI/data IPO candidate, but the latest credible coverage found no formal S-1 or confirmed confidential filing.
ipoTracker.note: Earlier 2026 coverage said the company was not immediately prepping an IPO after a large funding round.

latestArticle.title: Databricks CEO says SaaS isn't dead, but AI will soon make it irrelevant

ipoTracker.process: Some IPO trackers claim Scale AI filed an S-1 in March 2026, but this snapshot did not find a matching company announcement, SEC filing, or major-news confirmation.
ipoTracker.note: Keep on the watchlist and require SEC/company/top-tier confirmation before marking as filed.

latestArticle.title: Is Scale AI Going Public? IPO & Stock Info

partnershipTracker.status: Anthropic is using Colossus 1 compute capacity for Claude workloads.
partnershipTracker.terms: Confirmed compute partnership; reported terms are up to $1.25B per month through May 2029.
partnershipTracker.note: This is the clearest example of a direct AI rival renting compute from another AI lab's infrastructure stack.

partnershipTracker.status: Anthropic expanded its AWS collaboration for Claude training and deployment.
partnershipTracker.terms: Up to 5GW of AWS capacity, including Trainium2 and Trainium3 capacity; AP reported a $100B+ AWS spending commitment over 10 years.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by company and major wire coverage
partnershipTracker.note: AWS remains Anthropic's primary cloud provider and training partner.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic and Amazon expand collaboration for up to 5 gigawatts of new compute

partnershipTracker.status: Anthropic signed for multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity through Google and Broadcom.
partnershipTracker.terms: Capacity expected to come online starting in 2027; coverage reports roughly 3.5GW in expanded TPU capacity.
partnershipTracker.note: This sits alongside, rather than replacing, Anthropic's AWS capacity.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, model distribution, investment, and GPU optimization
partnershipTracker.status: Claude is available through Microsoft Foundry and scaled on Azure using NVIDIA systems.
partnershipTracker.terms: Microsoft and NVIDIA committed up to $15B combined investment; Anthropic committed to buy $30B of Azure compute capacity.
partnershipTracker.note: This made Claude available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft cloud platforms.

latestArticle.title: Microsoft, NVIDIA and Anthropic announce strategic partnerships

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, product distribution, and IP licensing
partnershipTracker.status: Microsoft remains OpenAI's primary cloud partner, but OpenAI can now serve products across other clouds.
partnershipTracker.terms: Microsoft retains non-exclusive license rights to OpenAI models and products through 2032.
partnershipTracker.note: The amended agreement loosened OpenAI's prior exclusivity while keeping Azure first for supported capabilities.

latestArticle.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership

partnershipTracker.status: OpenAI and Amazon announced a multi-year strategic partnership for enterprise AI.
partnershipTracker.terms: Partnership expands OpenAI's reach beyond Azure-only channels and supports enterprise, startup, and consumer AI distribution.
partnershipTracker.note: This became easier after OpenAI's Microsoft partnership became less exclusive.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and Amazon announce strategic partnership

partnershipTracker.status: OpenAI and NVIDIA announced a strategic partnership for next-generation AI infrastructure.
partnershipTracker.terms: At least 10GW of NVIDIA systems, with first gigawatt expected on Vera Rubin in H2 2026.
partnershipTracker.note: Part of OpenAI's broad compute diversification across GPU and cloud providers.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and NVIDIA announce strategic partnership to deploy 10 gigawatts of NVIDIA systems

partnershipTracker.status: OpenAI agreed to deploy AMD GPUs for next-generation AI infrastructure.
partnershipTracker.terms: 6GW agreement across multiple generations of AMD Instinct GPUs.
partnershipTracker.note: The deal gives OpenAI a major non-NVIDIA GPU path.

latestArticle.title: AMD and OpenAI announce strategic partnership to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs

partnershipTracker.type: Data center and leased compute platform
partnershipTracker.status: Stargate functions as OpenAI's umbrella infrastructure platform with Oracle, SoftBank, CoreWeave, and related partners.
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI announced a 4.5GW Oracle partnership; later coverage says the structure shifted toward flexible leased compute.
partnershipTracker.note: Track this as a flexible infrastructure network rather than a single static joint venture.

latestArticle.title: Stargate advances with 4.5 GW partnership with Oracle

partnershipTracker.status: Meta deepened its CoreWeave partnership for AI cloud capacity.
partnershipTracker.terms: Approximately $21B in AI cloud capacity through December 2032.
partnershipTracker.note: A major example of hyperscalers and AI labs using neocloud capacity to accelerate infrastructure deployment.

latestArticle.title: Meta signs $21bn AI infrastructure contract with CoreWeave

partnershipTracker.status: CoreWeave announced a multi-year agreement to power Claude workloads.
partnershipTracker.terms: Financial terms were not disclosed in available coverage.
partnershipTracker.note: Part of Anthropic's broader multi-compute strategy.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave shares pop after Anthropic deal

partnershipTracker.status: Claude is deployed for U.S. government, intelligence, and defense workflows through Palantir and AWS infrastructure.
partnershipTracker.terms: Claude runs in Palantir AIP on accredited environments supported by AWS; separate DOD/agency contract context remains politically sensitive.
partnershipTracker.note: Track with care because DOD-related reporting includes disputes and changing authorization posture.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic and Palantir Partner to Bring Claude AI Models to AWS for U.S. Government Intelligence and Defense Operations
